mnemonics: IF ID EX MEM WB: I Imagine Eating Many Watermelons Backwards
Hazards Stall: Data, Control, Structural - Don't Call Security
keyPoints: Pipelining increases throughput by overlapping instruction execution stages
The five classic RISC pipeline stages are: Instruction Fetch (IF), Instruction Decode (ID), Execute (EX), Memory Access (MEM), Write Back (WB)
Pipeline hazards (data, control, structural) can cause stalls and reduce performance
commonMistakes: Assuming pipelining decreases the execution time of a single instruction (it doesn't, it increases throughput)
Forgetting to account for pipeline stalls and hazards when calculating speedup
Confusing data forwarding (bypassing) with solving all data hazards (it only reduces stalls)
